bp_title: Implement timeout mechanisms on agentic workflows
question_stem: GENREL03 — How do you implement remediation actions for generative AI workload loops, retries, and failures? (verified 2026-06-15 from genrel03.html)
verbatim_text: GENREL03-BP02 — Implement timeout mechanisms on agentic workflows. Apply wall-clock timeouts to agent runs and to the external compute / tool calls they invoke (accounting for cold starts), so a workflow that hangs (waiting on a slow or unresponsive tool, or looping without converging) is terminated rather than left running indefinitely. Pair timeouts with dead-letter queues for work that exceeds its deadline and with graceful termination so partial state is cleaned up. (Verified 2026-06-15 from genrel03.html; parked same session, unparked + promoted 2026-06-22.)
outcome_note: PROMOTED 2026-06-22 → GR3B2-01 (Don't let a stalled agent hang forever). 24th standard; second under GENREL03; reliability twin of GC5B1-01. Unparked from 2026-06-15. step_promotion 3/3/3/3. STANDALONE (user-directed over folding into GC5B1-01). Distinct failure: wall-clock deadline catches an I/O hang that GC5B1-01's step/token cap is blind to; fires when nothing returns (unlike GR3B1-01); run-level across a model-chosen tool sequence (unlike base-WAF REL05-BP05 per-call timeout). Scope kept NARROW (user 'Option 1'): deadline + graceful termination + DLQ + safe-termination defaulting to no-auto-retry; tool-call idempotency split out as a future standard. Artefact = agent run config (not prompt YAML). Contract: wall_clock_deadline + per-tool timeout + on_timeout(terminate/dead_letter) + central harness applies by construction + 3 pre-merge lints + quarterly review. Enforcement limit (wired-in not runtime) appended to enforcement_limits.md. impact_level High. RI at data/ri/GR3B2-01/README.md. Catalogue at 24 standards.
open_items: Future tool-call idempotency standard (would relax safe-termination lint). AWS BP step page reconstructed — verify numbered steps from live page. AIGP IV.C unverified. S3 re-upload + frontend build.
next_candidates: GENREL04 (prompt management) — next. Then GENREL05 (distributed availability), GENREL06 (distributed compute).
